name: ICNA Council for Social Justice
mission: To systematically facilitate assertive Muslim involvement in the field of human struggle for the rights of the poor and oppressed.
scoreSummary: Icna Council for Social Justice shows strong impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: ICNA Council for Social Justice is listed as "Zakat eligible" on Feeling Blessed. Their website also features a "Zakat Calculator - ICNA CSJ" and mentions "Zakat Qualification". Furthermore, they explicitly discuss how zakat should be given in America to the poor. (Source: https://www.feelingblessed.org/icnacsj)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

all_citations.claim: The charity has a score of 86.5/100, a program expense ratio of 82.6%, and 7.3 months of working capital.
Total revenue is $763,582 and the mission involves facilitating Muslim involvement in the struggle for the rights of the poor.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a respectable score due to its high financial efficiency and clear alignment with Islamic charitable pillars, though its overall impact score is moderated by its focus on capacity building rather than direct service delivery.
areas_for_improvement: Limited external data regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics for its programs
Opportunity to increase total revenue to scale national advocacy efforts

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is considered high for Muslim donors as the organization is Zakat-eligible and addresses religiously significant causes related to social justice and oppression [2]. The mission focuses on mobilizing the American Muslim community, creating a strong cultural and religious fit for its target donor base [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates effective financial management with a total revenue of $763,582 and a high program expense ratio of 82.6% [1][3]. While it maintains a strong financial position with 7.3 months of working capital, its impact is primarily focused on capacity building and advocacy, which results in a medium-high impact score [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The ICNA Council for Social Justice empowers the Muslim community to advocate for the rights of the poor and oppressed through systematic social justice initiatives.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 82.6%, ensuring the majority of funds support direct advocacy and services
Strong fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.10 per $1 raised
Solid liquidity with 7.3 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: The ICNA Council for Social Justice (ICNA CSJ) is a non-profit organization dedicated to facilitating assertive Muslim involvement in the struggle for human rights [3]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through supported social justice programs [2]. With a Charity Navigator score of 86.5/100, the council demonstrates a strong commitment to financial health and accountability [1].

all_citations.source_name: Charity Navigator - ICNA Council for Social Justice

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization needs to <cite id="1">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for a more accurate assessment of cost-effectiveness.
Developing a <cite id="1">clear theory of change</cite> with explicit causal pathways would help donors better understand how advocacy leads to long-term social shifts.

confidence.notes: Strong financial data but limited impact metrics.

credibility.explanation: Donors can rely on the organization's <cite id="1">86.5/100 overall Charity Navigator score</cite> and its <cite id="3">transparent 2023 Form 990 filing</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To improve credibility, the charity should address gaps in its <cite id="1">accountability beacon</cite> and seek to meet all <cite id="1">BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards</cite>.

rich_narrative.headline: The ICNA Council for Social Justice empowers the Muslim community to advocate for the rights of the poor and oppressed through systematic social justice initiatives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to empower the American Muslim community through systematic advocacy and are comfortable supporting a high-growth, lean organization.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through social justice and advocacy
Value high program efficiency where 99% of funds go to the mission
Care about the long-term rights of the poor and oppressed in the US
giving_considerations: The organization is in a growth phase with a 22.7% CAGR
Impact is measured through capacity building rather than direct aid counts
Governance is strong with 100% independent board members
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate, high-volume humanitarian aid statistics or those who require rigorous RCT-level evidence of impact.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified program activity and a documented theory of change, but a lack of specific beneficiary outcome data.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ICNA CSJ uses capacity building and advocacy to empower the Muslim community to secure rights for the marginalized.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts translate into tangible policy changes or community improvements.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 22.7% growth rate and a focus on national advocacy, the organization has significant capacity to absorb more funding.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an extremely lean <cite id="3">82.6% program expense ratio</cite> and a focus on <cite id="3">systematic involvement</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization directs a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 82.6%</cite> toward its core mission, far exceeding industry benchmarks for lean operations.
ICNA CSJ demonstrates remarkable efficiency, spending only <cite id="1">$0.09 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
The charity maintains <cite id="1">7.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient buffer for its ongoing advocacy programs.

strengths_deep_dive: ICNA CSJ exhibits a highly disciplined financial model where nearly every dollar raised is deployed into program activities. Their <cite id="3">82.6% program expense ratio</cite> is a standout metric for a capacity-building organization. Furthermore, the leadership maintains <cite id="1">100% independent board members</cite>, ensuring that governance remains objective and focused on the mission. This lean structure is complemented by a <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 Leadership & Adaptability score</cite> from Charity Navigator, suggesting the organization is well-positioned to respond to emerging social justice challenges.
rich_narrative.summary: The ICNA Council for Social Justice (ICNA CSJ) operates as a specialized advocacy and social service arm of the Islamic Circle of North America. The organization focuses on <cite id="3">facilitating assertive Muslim involvement</cite> in the struggle for the rights of the poor and oppressed. Unlike charities focused solely on immediate relief, ICNA CSJ emphasizes <cite id="1">systematic social justice initiatives</cite> and capacity building within the American Muslim community. The organization has demonstrated significant financial growth, maintaining a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 22.7%</cite>, which indicates a rapidly expanding donor base and increasing operational scale. For the 2023 tax year, the council reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $834,440</cite>, all of which was derived from contributions. This growth supports their mission to address systemic issues through <cite id="1">advocacy and community empowerment</cite>. The organization maintains a <cite id="1">three-star rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a solid foundation of accountability. By bridging the gap between faith-based values and civic action, ICNA CSJ provides a unique platform for donors who want their contributions to address the root causes of inequality while remaining <cite id="2">fully zakat-eligible</cite>.
